tagline: The street taco moment, no line required.
gap_rationale: Gracias Madre and WeHo's taqueria-adjacent crowd obsess over birria consommé and braised-beef depth. Chipotle's barbacoa has the right flavor bones — braised, chipotle-heavy, cumin-forward — but is never positioned as birria. Reframing the bowl with a consommé-style layering of tomatillo red chili salsa under the barbacoa and serving it explicitly as a birria bowl meets the demand without a single new ingredient.
confidence_reason: birria trending at recent_4w=52.0 vs avg_12m=46.6 (+11.5% acceleration), peak of 69 signals headroom; barbacoa already on menu as the closest analog

tagline: All the corn-stand energy, none of the napkins.
ingredients: corn salsa (mild — corn, poblano, red onion, jalapeño, lime)
chicken al pastor (LTO — pineapple, achiote, chipotle)
queso blanco (white cheese, tomatillo, jalapeño, poblano)
guacamole (avocado, lime, cilantro, red onion, jalapeño)
gap_rationale: Gracias Madre's plant-forward Mexican menu leans into street-corn-adjacent flavors that WeHo diners expect at their fast-casual spots — charred corn, cotija-style cheese, lime, chili. Chipotle's corn salsa + queso blanco + al pastor together recreate that elote street-cart profile in bowl form, a combination the menu never surfaces explicitly. Positioning this as the 'elote bowl' gives WeHo locals the flavor hit they're already seeking.
confidence_reason: elote at recent_4w=20.8 vs avg_12m=16.9 (+23% acceleration), approaching its seasonal peak of 30; al pastor LTO creates urgency; zero new SKUs needed

tagline: North African heat meets the bowl you actually feel good about.
ingredients: braised lamb (garlic, onion, turmeric, chili powder)
gap_rationale: Crossroads Kitchen and Open Sesame built their WeHo followings on exactly this profile — braised lamb or spiced protein over legumes, finished with North African chili heat and fermented-vegetable brightness. CAVA has every component but has never assembled them into a named bowl that signals 'Lebanese-North African' explicitly. The lentil base is underused on CAVA's menu despite being one of the chain's most nutritionally distinctive offerings.
confidence_reason: harissa trend rising (recent_4w=7.2 vs avg_12m=7.1, peak=10 shows near-peak moment); lentils at recent_4w=47.0, strongly rising; both ingredients already in CAVA pantry

tagline: The salad Open Sesame charges $22 for — yours for less.
ingredients: mixed greens (butter lettuce, baby kale, spinach, heritage greens)
gap_rationale: Open Sesame and Crossroads Kitchen both serve fattoush-adjacent salads with sumac-forward dressing as a cornerstone of the Lebanese-Mediterranean experience WeHo diners seek. CAVA has sumac slaw and pita crisps — the two defining elements of a fattoush — but no bowl that foregrounds them together. The crazy feta adds the jalapeño-forward heat that differentiates it from a generic Greek salad and speaks to the WeHo palate that grew up on Crossroads' bold vegetable cooking.
confidence_reason: sumac at recent_4w=10.2, avg_12m=11.3, but classified rising with peak of 100 — directional signal strong even with recent slight dip; all ingredients in pantry

gap_rationale: Moonbowls in WeHo has built a loyal following serving Korean rice bowls to a health-conscious crowd that wants bold umami flavors in a clean format. Sweetgreen's KBBQ wrap already has apple kimchi sauce and KBBQ dressing in its pantry but has never assembled a full bowl concept around them. The crispy rice base — already on the Crispy Rice Bowl — adds the textural drama that makes Korean bibimbap worth ordering. This bowl explicitly names and owns the Korean bowl space Moonbowls proved has WeHo demand.
confidence_reason: kimchi at recent_4w=54.8 vs avg_12m=53.4, rising trend, peak=100 — strongest sustained trend in the dataset; Sweetgreen's KBBQ wrap proves in-house ingredient viability

tagline: The Japanese spa lunch you didn't know a salad chain could pull off.
gap_rationale: WeHo's health-conscious Japanese-fusion lane is underserved by chains — diners drive to Silver Lake or Santa Monica for omakase-adjacent lunch salads with acid-forward citrus dressings. Sweetgreen's existing Miso Glazed Salmon bowl is the closest thing on any chain menu, but its current citrus sesame vinaigrette can be repositioned with a yuzu-forward callout using the sesame citrus herb profile already in the CAVA pantry's flavor vocabulary (Sweetgreen uses sesame citrus as a dressing too). The nori sesame seasoning and arugula peppery base give this a Japanese izakaya-salad feeling that no competitor at this price point offers.
confidence_reason: yuzu at recent_4w=31.0 vs avg_12m=28.4 (+9.2% acceleration), trend rising, peak=37 — room to grow; confidence MID because yuzu is a callout/positioning play on existing citrus sesame dressing rather than a discrete pantry addition
